stance: Risk-on but disciplined: a measured analyst-revision (ARM) tilt on a diversified multi-cap core; trim where revisions are collapsing, keep momentum/value as confirmation only since both are flat this round.
experience_note: Only ARM is live this round (z_mom and z_val show no cross-sectional dispersion; net_flow is null), so I am running a deliberately small-turnover ARM tilt, not a rotation. What I am watching: whether my strong-revision keeps (LENSKART, ARVIND, COFORGE, CUB, HEXT) out-return my falling-revision exits (HINDPETRO, LT, RBLBANK, ADANIENSOL, RAMCOCEM) over the next 1-3 months, and whether conviction-IC is positive (did I size the eventual winners bigger). What would change my mind: if next round shows ARM had near-zero or negative forward IC on this universe, I cut the ARM tilt and lean harder on the diversified core; if momentum/value dispersion reappears, I re-weight toward those. Pre-registered, no hindsight. last_round_review is null (early round), so these are sized on conviction, not on a graded prior.
